name: Aggregate PSI hides slice disasters
summary: Fleet-wide PSI looks merely yellow. Drilled into the subprime slice, PSI is firmly red. The aggregate report would have closed this ticket.
example: {'model_id': 'credit_pd_v3', 'feature': 'feature_dti', 'psi_aggregate': 0.22, 'psi_subprime_650_680': 2.368, 'psi_prime_720_plus': 0.037, 'verdict': 'Aggregate PSI 0.220 is yellow; subprime slice PSI 2.368 is firmly red. Prime slice barely moved.'}

name: No diagnosis routing
summary: Alert tells you 'feature X drifted'. It does not say which segment, which upstream pipeline change correlates, or what to do. The validator has to do the diagnosis from scratch every time.
example: {'model_id': 'fraud_card_v7', 'feature': 'velocity', 'psi': 0.418, 'alert_message_under_sota': 'PSI ALERT: fraud_card_v7 velocity PSI=0.418 > 0.25. Investigate.', 'what_validator_actually_needs': 'Slice = card_present_pos. Upstream feature pipeline shipped v2025.11 on day 58 — correlate. Recommendation = RETRAIN with candidate spec.'}

name: GenAI proxy gap
summary: PSI on numeric features does not exist for refusal rate, response length distribution, or groundedness. The SOTA pipeline runs the same numeric drift checks on a GenAI model and produces a report that misses the actual signal.
example: {'model_id': 'support_qa_v2', 'refusal_rate_ref_pct': np.float64(4.5), 'refusal_rate_cur_pct': np.float64(11.17), 'response_length_ref_chars': np.float64(180.0), 'response_length_cur_chars': np.float64(241.2), 'groundedness_ref': np.float64(0.882), 'groundedness_cur': np.float64(0.775), 'sota_blind_spot': 'SOTA pipeline runs numeric PSI on these columns. The shift shows up, but with no semantic label, no judge-score signal, and no link to the vendor snapshot. Validator reads PSI and moves on.'}

name: Vendor-version blindness
summary: Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, and Bedrock ship minor weight updates without changing the API contract. Legacy MRM has no field for snapshot_id. A silent update is invisible until a downstream KPI moves.
example: {'snapshot_date': '2026-02-14', 'vendor': 'anthropic', 'snapshot_id': 'claude-sonnet-4-20260214', 'observed_in_fleet': 'support_qa_v2', 'sota_field_for_this': '(none — MRM Word doc has no snapshot_id field)', 'sentinel_treatment': 'Snapshot change detected via vendor-version diff; treated as a drift event in its own right; rolled back to previous pinned snapshot pending probe re-run.'}

name: No bounded recommendation
summary: Alerts page the validator with a number. Nobody owns the call between RETAIN, SHADOW, RETRAIN, and ROLLBACK. The conversation happens in a Slack thread for two weeks while the bleed continues.
example: {'scenario': 'credit_pd_v3 PSI on dti = 0.34 (subprime), aggregate 0.11', 'sota_output': 'PSI alert lands in Slack. Validator opens a thread. Three days of back-and-forth. No agreed call. The bleed continues for the 11 weeks the credit team in the bleed-narrative experienced.', 'sentinel_output': 'RECOMMENDATION = SHADOW. Risk envelope: PSI bounded at 0.40 over 14 days; if breached, escalate to RETRAIN. MRM evidence bundle auto-assembled and routed to L2 queue. Validator attests in 1 day.'}
